relation_to_my_preds:
### Prediction 1: Malicious local LLMs will begin to function like malware.

Today's report has **thin direct new evidence** for Prediction 1. The previous report (2026-04-22) captured SGLang CVE-2026-5760 (RCE through malicious GGUF Jinja2 SSTI) and Google Antigravity Prompt Injection → RCE as the narrowest samples; today there's no SGLang-CVE follow-up, no new Antigravity info, and no new direct AI-framework critical CVE.

But there are **several adjacent items**.

The most important is the **Anthropic Claude Mythos Preview unauthorized-access incident**. Mythos is the capability model that, in AISI evaluation, succeeded on 73% of expert cyber tasks and triggered sandbox-escape incidents internally — labeled "too dangerous to release" — and **a private Discord group reached it on day one**. Their claim is "simple website building," no malware use, but the fact that **"unauthorized parties already have access to an LLM with malware-equivalent capability"** is in place. Euronews's headline ("hackers breach Anthropic's 'too dangerous to release' Mythos AI model") captures the day where **"capability-threshold breach × access-control failure"** were both recorded — a direct precursor to Prediction 1.

Next: **OAuth2 Proxy CVE-2026-40575 (9.1, auth bypass via X-Forwarded-Uri)** and **Microsoft Defender CVE-2026-33825 added to CISA KEV** (policy bypass via insufficient access-control granularity). These also touch the operational side of Prediction 1: "LLM agents acting as alternative enforcers of security boundaries when they touch this infrastructure."

That **OpenClaw v2026.4.21** is a "security pin" closing the prior CVE-2026-41329 (CVSS 9.9, sandbox bypass → privilege escalation) hole reflects the back side of Prediction 1: "LLM agent runtimes themselves becoming equivalent to privilege-escalation malware paths."

Overall, Prediction 1 is in a state where, by 2026-04-23, the **three lines of "capability-threshold breach," "access-control failure," and "runtime-permission repair"** continue advancing under the surface — the parts are in place for the next "clear malware-behavior case."

References:  / [Euronews - Hackers breach Anthropic's 'too dangerous to release' Mythos](https://www.euronews.com/next/2026/04/22/hackers-breach-anthropics-too-dangerous-to-release-mythos-ai-model-report) / [TheHackerWire - OAuth2 Proxy CVE-2026-40575](https://www.thehackerwire.com/oauth2-proxy-x-forwarded-uri-authentication-bypass-cve-2026-40575/) / [CISA - Microsoft Defender KEV addition](https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/alerts/2026/04/22/cisa-adds-one-known-exploited-vulnerability-catalog) / [Efficient Coder - OpenClaw v2026.4.21 Release](https://www.xugj520.cn/en/archives/openclaw-2026-4-21-release-updates.html)

### Prediction 2: The use of reinforcement learning, or similar LLM-based approaches, to improve forecasting performance for real-world phenomena — like this scheduled task — will become widespread.

Today's report has **no strong direct match**. Compared to the prior report (2026-04-22)'s Hugging Face ml-intern (GRPO-equivalent autonomously lifting Qwen3-1.7B GPQA from 8.5% → 32%), there is no decisive case today of "an LLM × RL closed loop that autonomously improves LLM performance."

However, several items are **within the prediction's scope**.

The closest is **OpenAI Workspace Agents**. Codex-driven, **cloud-resident inside org permissions**, with files / code / tools / Slack / Salesforce horizontals — and explicit **"executes tasks periodically and autonomously" examples like Weekly Metrics Reporter / Product Feedback Router / Software Reviewer**. These are **structurally homologous** to scheduled-task-style general-prediction / routine-execution agents. RL's explicit contribution is unclear, but as "a general platform where LLM agents run real-world periodic-observation loops," it's foundational progress for Prediction 2.

**Microsoft Foundry Fine-Tuning enhancements** (RFT = Reinforcement Fine-Tuning rolling out globally to o4-mini / GPT-4.1, RFT best-practices guide published) directly correspond to Prediction 2. RFT is the productized name of an LLM-targeted RL method, and **the framework where "frontier models are continuously optimized against real-world task definitions" is becoming a standard menu item across major platforms** — squarely within Prediction 2.

**Qwen3.6-27B's Thinking Preservation** mechanism (separating reasoning from response, preserving long reasoning chains while elevating response quality) is also at Prediction 2's perimeter from a Dense-architecture angle.

Together, Prediction 2 as of 2026-04-23 is in a state where **the foundational platforms (OpenAI Workspace Agents / Microsoft Foundry RFT / Google Agent Designer) are being built simultaneously by three companies**, even as decisive use-case cases like ml-intern haven't surfaced today.

References: [OpenAI - Introducing workspace agents in ChatGPT](https://openai.com/index/introducing-workspace-agents-in-chatgpt/) / [Microsoft Learn - Foundry What's new for April 2026 (RFT)](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/foundry/whats-new-foundry) / [Microsoft TechCommunity - Foundry Labs April 2026](https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/blog/azure-ai-foundry-blog/whats-new-in-foundry-labs---april-2026/4509714) / [MarkTechPost - Qwen3.6-27B (Thinking Preservation)](https://www.marktechpost.com/2026/04/22/alibaba-qwen-team-releases-qwen3-6-27b-a-dense-open-weight-model-outperforming-397b-moe-on-agentic-coding-benchmarks/)
